aboutBlock.desc:
My name is Sergey, with 3+ years in frontend development. Currently a Middle+ Frontend Developer at Yandex, on the B2B payments team of Yandex 360 for Business (admin.yandex.ru).

Most of my time goes to product features that grow functionality and bring the business money. I own them end-to-end: from design and server-side SSR/BFF logic (Node.js) to production rollout and A/B experiments — a feature turnkey, not just the client.

On my own initiative I also remove team pains: speeding up infrastructure and CI, working on performance, the design system, tooling and analytics. This portfolio site is built on my work stack — a living example of what I can do.

block1.desc: SPA and SSR applications with React/Next.js, server-side BFF logic in Node.js. Adaptive layouts (CSS Modules, SCSS, Tailwind), performance tuning (APDEX, TTFB, Lighthouse).

block2.desc: React + TypeScript, state management (Redux/Zustand/Effector), design systems and UI kits. I ship features to A/B in production. Testing: Playwright, Jest + RTL.

block3.desc: Figma prototyping, adaptive grids, micro-interactions (Framer Motion). Knowledge of WCAG principles. Can explain why 'pretty' ≠ 'usable'.

block4.desc: Git (branching, rebase, conflicts), Code Review, ESLint/Prettier. Task decomposition in Jira/YouTrack. Docker for local dev environment.

block1.name: Gleb Kuznetsov
block1.review: Sergey is incredibly easy to work with 🔥 He closes tasks fast and with quality, sometimes even ahead of QA — you can tell he thinks a task through instead of just writing code and tossing it over the fence. If bugs do show up, they don't survive half a day. He also writes great autotests, which is a rare talent for a frontender. I'd happily work with him more.

block2.name: Timur Safin
block2.review: Sergey was super responsive when I asked for help contributing to our service — with his help we closed the task noticeably faster. Thanks!

block3.name: Alisa Kovaleva
block3.review: A responsible and reliable developer who pays real attention to copy — that's genuinely great. Always reachable: happy to hop on a call, walk through a flow, discuss texts and design. Even on tight deadlines he makes edits carefully, never mixing anything up. Communication is comfortable and transparent — as a UX writer I'm very happy with the quality of his work 😃

block4.name: Anton Lebedev
block4.review: Working with you on projects is really great! Easy to communicate, you dive in well and are always ready to dig into a question instead of brushing it off. One wish — don't work on weekends 😄

block5.name: Pavel Morozov
block5.review: A real pleasure to deal with. Within a project, communication is easy: he explains complex things clearly and lays out risks and timelines. What to improve? Nothing really — all great.

block3.h: Sped up team CI: −30% p90, ~2400 hrs/year

block4.h: Talk at an internal frontend conference

block1.desc:
Building the B2B admin panel of Yandex 360 for Business (admin.yandex.ru) in a product frontend team. I own product features end-to-end: design, alignment with adjacent teams, development, production rollout and A/B.

Product work (~85% of my time) — growing functionality and business revenue:
• Delivered large cross-service features turnkey: new registration flows, add-on activation, referral and promo mechanics — part already in production.
• I own features end-to-end: frontend + server-side SSR/BFF logic (Node.js) + integrations with adjacent services, not just the client.
• Confirmed A/B: conversion to add-on activation grew 6.5×, to first top-up 1.5×.
• Cut TTM for new locales by 5–7% and Dev Time by 20–40% via modular refactoring.
• Contributed components from scratch to the corporate design system (via Figma) and migrated the UI to it at scale.

Self-initiated extras (beyond product tasks):
• Sped up the team's PR flow: test-stand provisioning −19% (p50) / −30% (p90), ~2400 hrs/year saved.
• Tuned a heavy SSR endpoint from ~2.9s to ~200ms (−90%), raised APDEX.
• Built an instant on-stand debugging tool and analytics dashboards (ClickHouse + DataLens), gave a talk at an internal frontend conference, turned an analysis of 2000+ review comments into team ESLint rules.

Stack: React, TypeScript, Node.js (SSR/BFF), Redux, design system, CI/CD, Playwright/Jest, ClickHouse/DataLens

block2.desc:
Developed a multi-page Next.js SPA: SSR/SSG, dynamic routes, multilingual support (i18n).

Implemented auth and a role-based access system (JWT), interactive dashboards with charts (Chart.js), custom UI components. Set up CI/CD on GitHub Actions.

Stack: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Redux Toolkit, Vite, MUI, CSS Modules

block3.desc:
Built an admin panel for a mobile app together with a Middle+ frontend developer.

Complex forms and validation, reusable UI components, build optimization, code reviews and joint codebase refactoring.

Stack: React, TypeScript, Redux Toolkit, PrimeReact, Webpack

block4.desc:
Taught beginner courses: JavaScript fundamentals and algorithms, DOM and browser APIs, hands-on tasks on real cases.

Honed the ability to explain complex concepts simply.

block5.desc:
Commercial freelance: landing pages and SPAs with Vue/React, Three.js experiments (3D graphics).

Working with client requirements and deadlines, rapid adoption of new tech.

Stack: Vue 2/3, React, JavaScript, Tailwind

block1.name: Bauman Moscow State Technical University
block1.desc:
Advanced computer science: OOP and SOLID principles, algorithms and data structures (hash tables, graphs, trees, Big-O notation).
Applied mathematics: matrix operations, linear regression, data interpolation/extrapolation (implemented in JavaScript/TypeScript).
Low-level knowledge: computer architecture, memory management, performance optimization.
Networking: HTTP/HTTPS, WebSockets, REST API principles.
Concurrent programming: multithreading, asynchronicity (Event Loop).

block2.name: Gymnasium №7
block2.desc:
Basic programming in C++ and Python.
Robotics: microcontroller programming (Arduino, LEGO Mindstorms).
Introduction to Linux systems and PC hardware.
Math and CS olympiad participation.

projects.text1:
The B2B admin panel of Yandex 360 for Business is a large team product (a frontend team of 15+ engineers) that has existed for years. I'm one of the developers; below is what I personally owned.

Main work (~85% of my time) — product projects that grow functionality and business revenue:
- Large cross-service features turnkey: new registration flows, add-on activation, referral and promo mechanics
- I own features end-to-end: frontend + server-side SSR/BFF logic (Node.js) + integrations with adjacent services
- Confirmed A/B: conversion to add-on activation 6.5×, to first top-up 1.5×
- Cut TTM for new locales by 5–7% and Dev Time by 20–40%
- Contributions to the corporate design system (components from scratch via Figma)
projects.text2:
Self-initiated extras (beyond product tasks):

1. Sped up the team's PR flow: test-stand provisioning −19% (p50) / −30% (p90), ~2400 hrs/year saved
2. Performance: a heavy SSR endpoint 2.9s → 200ms (−90%), higher APDEX
3. An instant on-stand debugging tool + analytics dashboards (ClickHouse + DataLens)
4. A talk at an internal frontend conference, team ESLint rules from an analysis of 2000+ reviews

Stack: React, TypeScript, Node.js (SSR/BFF), Redux, design system, CI/CD, Playwright/Jest, ClickHouse/DataLens

projects.text1:
Large-scale Next.js app (50+ pages) with key technologies:

- Full JWT auth with localStorage token storage
- RBAC system with permission tiers
- i18n multilingual support (RU/EN/EE)
- Component architecture: custom + adapted Material UI
- Data visualization with Chart.js (line charts, doughnuts)
- Interactive tables with dataset filtering
- State: Redux + Redux Thunk for async ops
- Optimizations: SWR caching, lazy loading, SSR
- Testing: Jest unit tests
- SEO: Lighthouse-optimized
- Custom Webpack build
- CI/CD: Docker + Nginx, GitHub Actions auto-deploy
projects.text2:
Key achievements:

1. Implemented high-load auth system with JWT + RBAC
2. Optimized performance via SSR + code-splitting (Lazy Loading)
3. Built comprehensive dashboard analytics with Chart.js
4. Configured full CI/CD pipeline with Docker
5. Achieved 90+ Lighthouse scores
6. Implemented multilingual dynamic translations

Stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Redux Toolkit, RTK Query, Material UI v5, Chart.js 4, i18next, Jest, Docker, Nginx

projects.text1:
Cross-platform React Native app (Expo) mirroring web functionality:

- Full sync with Next.js web version
- JWT auth adaptation for mobile
- Same RBAC system with mobile UX considerations
- Shared i18n config
- Custom solutions for unsupported libraries:
  - Native module modifications
  - Web API polyfills
  - Critical library rewrites (Chart.js → Victory Native)
- Low-end device optimizations
projects.text2:
Technical challenges & solutions:

1. Migration from web-specific libraries:
   - Material UI → React Native Paper + custom
   - Chart.js → customized Victory Native
   - Redux Persist → AsyncStorage with serialization

2. Native enhancements:
   - Gestures for interactive charts
   - Large dataset optimizations
   - Custom nav stack (React Navigation 6)

3. Implementation:
   - Single codebase for iOS/Android
   - Offline mode with critical data caching
   - Expo Notifications for push
   - Biometric auth

Stack: React Native 0.72, Expo SDK 48, TypeScript, Victory Native, React Navigation 6, Redux Toolkit, i18next, Jest

projects.text1:
Custom-stack SPA resume with features:

- Architecture: Vite + React (no UI libraries)
- 100% custom components (Zero Dependency UI)
- Performance:
  - Vite build (5x faster than Webpack)
  - Low-end server optimized (1GB RAM)
  - Code splitting + tree shaking
- i18n:
  - Dynamic language loading (RU/EN)
  - SEO-optimized meta tags per language
- Interactivity:
  - Custom SwiperJS implementation
  - Complex animations via Animate.css
  - Pure CSS parallax
projects.text2:
Technical highlights:

1. Performance:
   - Build time <15s (vs 2.5min Webpack)
   - Lighthouse: 98/100 (PWA-ready)
   - Lazy-loaded heavy assets

2. Unique implementations:
   - Adaptive grid system (no libraries)

3. Animations/UX:
   - Micro-animations for all interactive elements
   - Smooth page transitions

Stack: Vite 5, React 18, TypeScript, i18next, Animate.css, SwiperJS 11, CI/CD (GitHub Actions)

projects.text1:
Next.js rental platform with full SSR and:

- Architecture: 100% server-side data (except PATCH/POST)
- UI Kit: Deeply customized Mantine UI
- Image optimizations:
  - Custom SwiperJS (v11) carousel
  - Next.js Image + lazy loading
  - Content priority loading
- Security: Google Authenticator for admin (TOTP)
- Validation: Zod + React Hook Form schemas
- SEO: 95+ Lighthouse (SSR + dynamic meta tags)
projects.text2:
Admin system & tech details:

1. Secure admin panel:
   - TOTP auth (google-authenticator)
   - Short-TTL JWT tokens
   - Custom data validation (including files)

2. Optimizations:
   - Dynamic SwiperJS import
   - Critical route prefetching
   - API response caching (stale-while-revalidate)

3. Integrations:
   - Tabler Icons (custom bundle)
   - Server Actions for mutations
   - Streaming SSR for slow connections

4. Implementation:
   - Zero client-side state (server cache)
   - Progressive enhancement for old browsers
   - Full TypeScript strict mode

Stack: Next.js 14 (App Router), Mantine UI 7, SwiperJS 11, Zod, React Hook Form, Google Authenticator, Tabler Icons, Turbopack

projects.text1:
High-performance Next.js landing with full infra:

- Next.js 14 SSR for instant loading
- Auto-generated SEO meta tags
- Media optimizations:
  - Compressed WebP images
  - Below-the-fold lazy loading
- Full infrastructure:
  - Docker containerization
  - Nginx reverse proxy with HTTP/2
  - Let's Encrypt via Certbot
- CI/CD pipeline:
  - GitHub Actions auto-deploys
  - Pre-deploy build checks
  - Dependency caching
projects.text2:
Technical implementation:

1. Performance:
   - 100/100 Google PageSpeed
   - TTFB < 200ms via SSR
   - Optimized web fonts

2. Security:
   - Auto SSL renewal
   - CSP security headers
   - Nginx DDoS rate-limiting

3. DevOps:
   - Multi-stage Docker builds
   - Container health checks
   - Service auto-recovery

Stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Docker 24, Nginx 1.25, Certbot 2.8, CI/CD (GitHub Actions)

projects.text1:
Innovative React+Vite app selling myself expensively but discounted:

- Architecture: 'A4-first design' (no extra pixels!)
- UI Kit: CSS modules + 3hrs fighting margin: 0 auto
- Optimization: PDF generation without Word (because 'I'm a frontender, not office plankton')
- Security: i18n protects against non-English-speaking HR
- Validation: 'Download' button checks for PDF reader
- SEO: 100 Lighthouse (because there's nothing but CV)
projects.text2:
Technical quirks:

1. Foolproofing:
   - 'Change language' requires confirmation ('Sure? You only learned English at school!')
   - PDF generator ignores tyres (magic 'AutoBeauty' algo)

2. Optimizations:
   - Lazy-loaded Word jokes
   - Prefetched future projects (none exist yet)
   - Cached skill confidence (TTL = ∞)

3. Integrations:
   - GitHub API for star counts (0 → but I'm optimistic!)
   - LinkedIn API for 'Open to Work' (self-initiated)

4. Implementation:
   - 90% time spent on PDF rendering (but not Word!)
   - 10% convincing myself 'this isn't overengineering'
   - 0% documentation (but ReadME exists!)

Stack: React 18, Vite 5, i18n (for 'Me/Not Me' toggle), PDF-lib (Word revenge), 3 coffee cups.

workExampleModal.ndaNotice: The product UI is under NDA, so screenshots aren't published here. The product is publicly available at admin.yandex.ru.
